California
Fair P01itical
Practices Cornmission
March 29, 1990
Uelinda Guzman-}loore
Diepenbrock, wuLff, Plant
& Hannegan
P. O. Box 3034
Sacranento, CA 9 5812 -3 03 4
Re: Your Request for Advice
our File No. A-90-2 09
Dear lts . Guzman-I,loore :
You have requested advice concerning application of the
confl ict-of- interest provisions of the Political Reform Act (the
nActn)1 to your dutiel as a candidate for the Davis city council.
OUESTION
under the Act, does a conflict of interest exist when an as-
sociate attorney is on the city council with a partner of the law
firn for which she works?
CONCLUSION
Under the Act, it is not a conflict, of interest for you and a
partner of the law firro for which you work to be on the city
council together. Since our advice is linited only to provisions
of the Act, please contact the Attorney General's office and the
Davis City Attorney to deternine if this presents a conflict under
any other provisions of law.
FACTS
You are a candidate for the upcouing Davis city council elec-
tion on June 5, 1990. You are an associate attorney t ith the firn
of Diepenbrock, t{ulff , Plant & Hannegan in sacrauento, california.
ur. pavia Rosenberg, a partner of this law firn, is a member of
the Davis City Council.
1 coraarrrant code sections
are to the Government code
81000-91015. Alt statutory references
unless othenrise indicated.
428 J Street, Suite 8OO . P.O. Box 8O7 . Sacramento CA 95804{807 . (916)322.5660


File No. A-90-2 09
Page 2
ANAI,YS I S
The Act prohibits a public official 
from rnaking, participat-
ing in, or using his official 
position to influence a governnental
decision in which he knows or has reason to know he has a
financial interest. 
(section 87100.) A public official 
has a
financial interest in a decision if it is reasonably foreseeable
that the decisi,on wilL have a naterial financial effect,
distinguishable fron its effect on the public Arenerally, on the
official or a menber of his inrnediate fanily or on, anong other
things :
(a) Any business entity in which the public
official 
has a direct or indirect i.nvestment worth
one thousand dollars ($1000) or Dore.
(b) Any real property in which the public
official 
has a direct or indirect interest worth
one.thousand dollars (S1000) or Eore.
(c) Any source of income, other than gifts
and other than loans by a comnercial lending
institution in the regular course of business on
terms available to the public without regard to
official status, aggregating two hundred fifty 
do1-
lars ($250) or nore in value provided to, received
by or pronised to the public official within 12
nonths prior to the tiue uhen the decision is made.
(d) Any business entity in which the public
official 
is a director, officer, partner, trustee,
employee, or holds any position of managenent.
(e) Any donor of, or any internediary or
agent for a donor of, a gift or gifts aggregating
two hundred fifty dollars (5250) or nore in value
provided to, received by, or pronised to the public
official within 12 months prior to the tine vhen
the decision is made.
(Section 87103 . )
Under the Act, a conflict of interest is based upon the
existence of a public offLcial.rs financial interest in a decision.
lhe facts of the situation you have presented do not involve a
financial interest and therefore do not invoke the Jurisdiction of
the Act.
However, other provisions of lal, Day apply. Therefore,
please contact the Attorney Generalrs office regarding such mat-
ters as the Brorrn Act, and contact the Davis City Attorney regard-
ing any loca1 ordinances which nay apply your situation. -


Fi■e No. A-90-209
Page 3

322-590■.
Sincere■y′
Kathryn E. Donovan
